title: Mapping and Continuous Monitoring of the European AI Ecosystem
client: European Commission, Directorate-General for Communications Networks, Content and Technology (DG CNECT)
key_requirements: Systematic identification and mapping of all AI-relevant organizations across all 27 EU member states
Coverage of AI system developers, deployers, GPAI model providers, conformity assessment bodies, and public sector bodies
Classification of organizations by AI risk category (per AI Act Annex III), sector, geography, company size, and role in the AI value chain
Mapping of supply chain and subcontracting relationships between AI developers and deployers, including cross-border dependencies
Continuous monitoring capability: monthly new entrant identification, quarterly portfolio change detection, quarterly M&A tracking, semi-annual concentration alerts, annual trend analysis

Methodology must go beyond commercial registries and self-reported classifications; must identify organizations via technical signals (job postings, API offerings, procurement notices, patent filings, etc.)
Evidence-based classification derived from observable, verifiable sources (website content, technical documentation, procurement records, etc.)
Quality assurance and false-positive rejection procedures must be described
Full methodological transparency and reproducibility; all steps documented for independent replication
Inception Report submitted within 6 weeks of contract signature and approved before substantive work begins
Core organization list refreshed quarterly; full indicator profiles updated annually
Ad-hoc expedited update requests addressed within 10 working days
Structured, machine-readable datasets (JSON/CSV) with accompanying data dictionaries
Delivery of four sector deep-dive analytical reports on high-risk AI sectors selected by the Commission
Capacity building for DG CNECT staff including methodology documentation, training sessions, and at least two facilitated workshops
Project manager with EU-funded research or consultancy experience of comparable scope
At least one methodological expert in web-based or AI-driven organizational data collection and classification
At least one sector specialist with expertise in EU digital policy and the AI Act
Data engineering capacity to maintain and deliver structured datasets at required update frequency
At least one expert in SME and industrial ecosystem analysis in the EU context
Subcontracting arrangements permitted with appropriate disclosure
summary: The European Commission's DG CNECT is procuring a service contract to systematically map and continuously monitor the European AI ecosystem across all 27 EU member states, in support of AI Act implementation and EU AI policy. The contractor must identify, classify, and profile AI-relevant organizations — including developers, deployers, GPAI providers, and conformity assessment bodies — using evidence-based, reproducible methodologies that capture actors not visible in standard commercial databases. Key deliverables include a baseline dataset, quarterly monitoring reports, sector deep-dives, annual trend analyses, and a capacity-building package for Commission staff, all to be delivered over a maximum period of 36 months for an estimated value of EUR 3,200,000.
